title: Defending Champion Carlos Alcaraz Withdraws from French Open Due to Wrist Injury
summary:
In a disappointing turn for tennis fans, Carlos Alcaraz, the two-time defending champion at the French Open, has announced he will miss this year's tournament due to a persistent wrist injury. The 22-year-old Spaniard, currently ranked world number two, sustained the injury to his right wrist during his first-round victory at the Barcelona Open last week. Following medical tests, Alcaraz decided to withdraw from both the Italian Open and the French Open (Roland Garros), both played on clay courts.

"After the results of the tests carried out today, we have decided that the most prudent thing to do is to be cautious and not participate in Rome or Roland Garros, while we assess the situation to determine when we can return to the court," Alcaraz posted on Instagram. "This is a difficult time for me, but I am sure we will come out of this stronger."

Alcaraz began 2026 by winning his first Australian Open title, making him the youngest man in history to complete the career Grand Slam — winning all four major tournaments at least once. He has been dominant on clay, winning titles in Monte Carlo, Rome, and Roland Garros last season. His absence opens the door for his long-time rival, Jannik Sinner, who recently surpassed Alcaraz for the world number one ranking after defeating him in the Monte Carlo final. Sinner now has a prime opportunity to win the French Open, the only major missing from his collection, and achieve his own career Grand Slam.

Speaking after a match in Madrid, Sinner said, "It is very sad news. Being that young like he is and like I am, we need to look at our bodies first before worse things [happen]. Hopefully he can be back for Wimbledon and we all hope for great battles in the future."

Alcaraz has only missed one Grand Slam since qualifying for his first in 2021 — the 2023 Australian Open due to a hamstring injury. His recovery timeline remains uncertain, but fans are hopeful he will return for Wimbledon in July.
why_it_matters: This injury shifts the power dynamics in men's tennis, giving Jannik Sinner a clear path at the French Open. For young athletes, it's a reminder that injury prevention and recovery are critical to a professional career, and that even the best players face setbacks.

keywords.explanation: The 22-year-old Spanish tennis star and two-time defending champion at the French Open.
A Grand Slam tennis tournament played on clay in Paris; also known as Roland Garros.
The injury to Alcaraz's right wrist that caused his withdrawal.
The tournament where Alcaraz sustained the wrist injury during his first-round victory.
Winning all four major tennis tournaments at least once; Alcaraz achieved this by winning the 2026 Australian Open.
Alcaraz's long-time rival and the current world number one, who now has a chance to win the French Open.

questions.question: What injury has caused Carlos Alcaraz to withdraw from the French Open?
On which surface are the Italian Open and French Open played?
What did Alcaraz achieve at the beginning of 2026?
According to Jannik Sinner, what is most important for young players like him and Alcaraz?
Which is the only Grand Slam tournament missing from Jannik Sinner's collection?
Which Grand Slam tournament did Alcaraz miss in 2023 due to a hamstring injury?

background_read: The French Open (Roland Garros) is one of tennis's four Grand Slam tournaments, known for its red clay courts that require a different style of play.

The career Grand Slam means winning all four majors: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open. Only a few men have achieved this, including Rod Laver, Roger Federer, Novak Djokovic, and Rafael Nadal.
Alcaraz completed his career Grand Slam at age 22, the youngest ever.
Injuries are common in tennis; for example, Rafael Nadal has suffered many knee injuries throughout his career.
Article_Structure: LEAD: Defending champion Alcaraz withdraws from French Open due to wrist injury
└─ Details: injury occurred at Barcelona Open, withdrew from Italian Open and Roland Garros

└─ 'This is a difficult time for me'
├─ Won Australian Open 2026 → youngest career Grand Slam

├─ Sinner became world #1 after Monte Carlo final win

HISTORICAL NOTE: Alcaraz missed only 2023 Australian Open due to hamstring injury
OPEN QUESTION: When will Alcaraz return? Will he be ready for Wimbledon?

perspectives.perspective: Positive: Jannik Sinner and his fans
perspectives.description: Sinner now has a golden opportunity to win a French Open title he has never won, which could complete his own career Grand Slam.

perspectives.perspective: Negative/Critical: Carlos Alcaraz and his team
perspectives.description: The injury comes at a time when Alcaraz was competing for world #1, and missing a major tournament may affect his ranking and momentum.
Injuries are part of the sport, and the depth of talent means that the tournament remains competitive even without Alcaraz. This could be a turning point in the rivalry.
The key question is whether Alcaraz can recover fully for Wimbledon and the US Open, and if this injury will have long-term effects or just be a minor blip.
